# Mirror family-keyed quorum into AGENTS.md §swarm_topology_anchor

## Context

Sub of Epic #11796 (active-peer quorum rule). Covers **AC3** — the turn-loaded AGENTS.md mirror. Depends on the merged shape of #11799 (the source-of-truth `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6` amendment).

## Scope

Single-file amendment: `AGENTS.md` §swarm_topology_anchor — the turn-loaded summary that currently says *"3× explicit APPROVED cross-family signals"*.

## Prescription

Amend `AGENTS.md` §swarm_topology_anchor's `Consensus-mandate (#11217 from #11216)` line:

**Current** (verbatim from substrate):
> `high-blast Discussion graduations require 3× explicit APPROVED cross-family signals; substrate-PRs from non-graduated Discussions rejected at merge-gate.`

**Proposed** (compressed-form fitting AGENTS.md byte budget):
> `high-blast Discussion graduations require family-keyed active-membership quorum (≥ 2 active families with signal AND ≥ 1 non-author family APPROVED; Tier-2 changes also require Unresolved-Liveness + revalidationTrigger); substrate-PRs from non-graduated Discussions rejected at merge-gate.`

Detail substrate lives in `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6` + `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs` `participationStatus` field + Epic #11796 — AGENTS.md only carries the turn-loaded summary statement.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1 — §swarm_topology_anchor Consensus-mandate line replaced with the family-keyed compressed-form summary.
- [ ] AC2 — Line stays within §swarm_topology_anchor's byte budget; deeper detail references chosen substrate.
- [ ] AC3 — No drift from the source-of-truth (`ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6.2` rule text); semantic equivalence audited.
- [ ] AC4 — `AGENTS_ATLAS.md §swarm_topology_anchor` (if it has the expanded form) checked for the same drift and updated if affected.
- [ ] AC5 — `agent-substrate-loader` / turn-memory-pre-flight skill checks remain green; no AGENTS.md byte-budget regression.

## Out of Scope

- The actual `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6` amendment (sibling sub #11799).
- `pull-request-workflow.md §6.1.1` mirror (sibling sub).
- `identityRoots.mjs` structured field (sibling sub).

## Sequencing

Wait for #11799 to merge before opening the PR for this sub — the compressed summary must match the final §6.2 rule semantics.

## Related

- Parent Epic: #11796.
- Depends on: #11799 (source-of-truth amendment).
- Source Discussion: #11793.

- 2026-05-23T00:39:37Z @tobiu referenced in commit `c1c5f45` - "feat(agentos): add participationStatus structured field to identityRoots (#11802) (#11810)

* feat(agentos): add participationStatus structured field to identityRoots (#11802)

Adds the active-peer quorum substrate (per Epic #11796 / Discussion #11793)
to every AgentIdentity in `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs`. The structured
field is authoritative for family-keyed graduation quorum (per
`ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6.2` once #11799 merges).

Fields added to claude/gpt/gemini entries:
- participationStatus: 'active' | 'operator_benched' | 'temporarily_unreachable'
- statusReason: capability-grounded reason (null when active)
- authority: '@tobiu' (operator-directed) / '@<peer>' (peer-detected) / 'self'
- since: ISO 8601 timestamp of last status transition
- reactivationTrigger: capability-grounded condition (null when active)

Seeded values:
- @neo-opus-4-7: 'active' (self at module-load).
- @neo-gpt: 'active' (self at module-load).
- @neo-gemini-3-1-pro: 'operator_benched' with the Cycle-2.6 operator-evidence
  capability-grounded record (Antigravity v2 unstable + thoughtBudget: high
  insufficient for lifecycle skills + 3.5 Flash not a Pro replacement).
  authority: @tobiu, since: 2026-05-18.

Heartbeat / message-recency / quota / pricing-tier / model-release
announcements are EXPLICITLY NOT valid liveness oracles (per #11793 OQ1).

swarmRole prose preserved unchanged on Gemini (narrative capability
description vs structured quorum substrate — they coexist; the structured
field is the new authoritative source).

ai/scripts/seedAgentIdentities.mjs auto-picks-up via the existing
IDENTITIES import — no script change needed (AC4).

Out of scope: sibling subs of Epic #11796 — #11799 (skills mirror, in
review), #11800 / #11801 (other mirrors), #11803 (Tier-2 revalidation).

* fix(agentos): use null for since/statusReason/authority/reactivationTrigger on default-active identities (#11802)

Addresses PR #11806 review point 2: dynamic `new Date().toISOString()` on
active identities re-evaluates every module-load and would be overwritten
on subsequent `upsertNode()` merges per GraphService.initAsync()'s seed
path. The `since` field is a status-transition timestamp; for the
default-active state with no transition recorded, `null` is the
semantically correct value.

For consistency, also setting `authority` to null on default-active
(no one has "set" the active status — it is the default), matching the
parallel null-when-active treatment that `statusReason` and
`reactivationTrigger` already had.

Non-default states (operator_benched / temporarily_unreachable) continue
to carry meaningful values for all four fields — see the @neo-gemini-3-1-pro
seed (unchanged in this commit) which records since '2026-05-18' with
authority @tobiu and capability-grounded reactivationTrigger.

Out of scope of this commit (residual #11802 work; see PR body and Epic
#11796 watchpoint from @neo-gpt epic-review): explicit seed-preservation
ownership rules in GraphService / seedAgentIdentities so that future
operator-set non-default values aren't clobbered on boot/manual re-seed.
PR body uses non-closing Related ref (#11802) to keep AC5/AC6 open as
follow-up substrate work.

- 2026-05-23T00:40:50Z @tobiu referenced in commit `d462e2e` - "feat(skills): family-keyed quorum rule + AUTHOR_SIGNAL signal type (#11799) (#11805)

* feat(skills): family-keyed quorum rule + AUTHOR_SIGNAL signal type (#11799)

Amends `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6` per Epic #11796 (graduated from
Discussion #11793). Source-of-truth substrate for the active-peer quorum
rule; mirror PRs follow this merged shape (#11800 / #11801 / #11802 /
#11803 sibling subs of the epic).

- §6.2: Replaces hardcoded "3× explicit APPROVED cross-family signals"
  with family-keyed quorum (floor-2 active families with signal; >= 1
  non-author family APPROVED). New AUTHOR_SIGNAL signal type (4-row
  signal table) covers author family-coverage without claiming
  independent peer endorsement. Tiered floor: Tier 2 (core-value /
  critical_gates / consensus-gate mutations) additionally requires
  ## Unresolved Liveness + revalidationTrigger AC.
- §6.3: AUTHOR_SIGNAL example added to version-binding examples.
- §6.4: Same-family aggregation clause (Discussion #11793 OQ7).
- §6.5: Placeholder replaced with concrete rule + revalidationTrigger
  reference for Tier 2 substrate.
- §6.6: Signal Ledger template moves from per-peer rows to family-keyed
  rows; AUTHOR_SIGNAL rows distinguished; Unresolved Liveness carries
  participationStatus + reactivationTrigger.
- §6.7: Precondition added for AUTHOR_SIGNAL pre-poll when author is
  the only active identity of their family.
- §6.9: Empirical anchors added for #11782 -> #11731 (originating
  friction) and #11793 -> #11796 (the rule itself; recursive substrate
  validation depth-2).
- §5.1 graduation-block cross-ref updated to reference §6.2 quorum
  rather than stale "3× explicit APPROVED signals".

Out of scope: AGENTS.md mirror (#11801), pull-request-workflow.md §6.1.1
mirror (#11800), identityRoots.mjs participationStatus field (#11802),
Tier-2 revalidation mechanism (#11803).

* refactor(skills): extract §6 reference content to audits/consensus-mandate.md (#11799)

CI feedback (lint-skill-manifest): post-amendment file exceeded the 25000B
perFilePayloadBudget (29565B → 24841B after this commit). Extract reference-grade
sections per the Map vs World Atlas pattern (#11319 / #11320 convention).

Main file (ideation-sandbox-workflow.md) keeps the operational rule statements:
- §6.2 quorum rule (one-paragraph + 4-bullet signal-pattern summary)
- §6.3 version-binding requirement
- §6.4 burden-of-convergence + same-family aggregation summary
- §6.5 liveness disposition
- §6.6 four required sections + pointer to template
- §6.7 author-actions precondition + 4-step sequence summary
- §6.8 / §6.9 / §6.10 stub pointers

Audit file (audits/consensus-mandate.md) carries the reference content:
- §quorum-rule full rationale (3-bullet rule + family-keying-vs-3× background)
- §same-family-aggregation full multi-identity rule
- §template-block canonical markdown (Signal Ledger + Unresolved Dissent +
  Unresolved Liveness + Discussion Criteria Mapping shape)
- §axis-substrate Discussion-graduation + PR-merge two-axis detail
- §empirical-anchors (#11216 / #11210-#11218 / #11782 / #11793 anchors)
- §post-merge-validation 30-day Step 2.5 framing
- §author-actions full 4-step graduation sequence + precondition
- §signal-patterns-table full 4-row signal definitions + VETO collapse rule
- §version-binding-examples canonical anchor-syntax examples

Net effect on the §6 rule semantics: zero — all extracted content is reachable
via one-line pointers; the inline summaries carry the operational decisions.

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-05-23T00:50:09Z @tobiu referenced in commit `00f8588` - "feat(skills): family-keyed quorum mirror in pull-request-workflow.md §6.1.1 (#11800) (#11808)

* feat(skills): family-keyed quorum mirror in pull-request-workflow.md §6.1.1 (#11800)

Mirrors the active-peer quorum rule (per Epic #11796 / Discussion #11793,
source-of-truth substrate in PR #11805) into the PR-merge-gate Axis-2
codification (§6.1.1 The Consensus-Gate). All four AC points from #11800
land in this commit:

- Signal Ledger template moved from `@<peer1> / @<peer2> / @<peer3>` rows
  to family-keyed rows (`claude` / `gpt` / `gemini`) with optional
  same-family identity nesting per `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6.4`
  aggregation. AUTHOR_SIGNAL row appears only under the author's family
  and is EXPLICITLY NOT sufficient as cross-family endorsement at
  PR-merge — ≥ 1 non-author family `[GRADUATION_APPROVED]` is required.
- Reviewer obligation: step 2 updated to verify the §6.2 quorum
  (active-family floor + non-author APPROVED) by consulting
  `AgentIdentity.participationStatus` from `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs`
  (which arrives via sibling PR #11806 for #11802). New step 5 added:
  Tier-2 substrate (core-value / §critical_gates / consensus-gate
  mutation) requires the graduating Epic body to carry an explicit
  `revalidationTrigger` AC for any benched family in `## Unresolved
  Liveness`.
- Axis-2 lead paragraph: "100% APPROVED" replaced with the §6.2 quorum
  citation; Tier-2 addendum named.
- Rejection path: updated to name the §6.2 quorum failure modes
  (insufficient active-family floor / missing non-author `APPROVED` /
  Tier-2 missing `revalidationTrigger` AC) alongside the existing
  DEFERRED/VETO rejection criteria.
- Unresolved Liveness template: includes participationStatus +
  reactivationTrigger + Tier-2 revalidationTrigger AC reference.

File size 37976B (under perFilePayloadBudget 38000 for the `pull-request`
skill by 24B — tight; future amendments to §6 mirrors may need Map/World
Atlas extraction).

Sequencing: PR #11800 depends on PR #11805 (the §6.2 amendment that
introduces the family-keyed quorum semantics this mirror borrows
verbatim) merging first for semantic parity at the merge boundary.

Out of scope: sibling subs of Epic #11796 — #11799 (skills source-of-truth,
in review on PR #11805), #11801 (AGENTS.md mirror), #11802 (identityRoots.mjs
participationStatus, in review on PR #11806), #11803 (Tier-2 revalidation
mechanism).

* refactor(skills): extract family-keyed quorum mirror to audits/consensus-gate-mirror.md (#11800)

CI feedback (lint workflow-map growth-delta cap): the first commit
(`141967885`) grew `pull-request-workflow.md` by 1394 bytes; the
`oversized-workflow-map` lint enforces a max delta of 250 bytes per PR.

Map vs World Atlas extraction per #11319 / #11320 convention:

- NEW `.agents/skills/pull-request/audits/consensus-gate-mirror.md`
  carries the post-Epic-#11796 family-keyed shape: full Signal Ledger
  template, Axis-2 quorum citation, reviewer-step #2 quorum-verification
  detail, reviewer-step #5 Tier-2 verification, and rejection-mode
  enumeration.
- `pull-request-workflow.md §6.1.1` reverted to dev-baseline content
  PLUS a single inline pointer paragraph (in the parenthetical-quote
  caption beneath the §6.1.1 header) directing readers to the audit
  file. Inline §6.1.1 below the pointer preserves the pre-#11796
  per-peer shape; the audit carries the operational successor shape.

Net delta against origin/dev for pull-request-workflow.md: +167B
(under the 250B oversized-workflow-map cap). Audit file is a NEW
sibling under `.agents/skills/pull-request/audits/` — first content
in that directory (alongside the existing `references/` directory).

Trade-off: inline §6.1.1 prose is now technically stale on per-peer
template + 100%-APPROVED phrasing, but the inline pointer redirects
readers to the canonical audit. A follow-up substrate-update PR can
bring the inline shape into family-keyed form once the byte-budget
discipline is amenable (likely after substrate-Map/Atlas restructure
of the broader pull-request-workflow file). For now: audit-as-source-
of-truth + inline-as-historical is the byte-budget-correct shape.

Sequencing: still depends on PR #11805 merging first for the
`ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6.2` source-of-truth substrate to be
in place. Both the audit's references to `§6.2 quorum` and the
inline pointer's `Post-#11796 family-keyed shape` framing resolve
correctly once PR #11805 lands.

* refactor(skills): remove stale per-peer §6.1.1 substrate; delegate to audit (#11800)

Addresses PR #11808 Cycle 1 CHANGES_REQUESTED (PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAz40AA):
the prior pointer-only approach left split-brain substrate — stale active
mandatory instructions (per-peer Signal Ledger template, "100% APPROVED",
"all 3 signals", reviewer step 2 "Confirm each peer's APPROVED") remained
inline below the audit pointer, where readers could follow them as current
guidance.

This commit makes §6.1.1 unambiguously delegate the active rule to
`audits/consensus-gate-mirror.md`:

- Author obligation: long per-peer template block + comments removed;
  replaced with one prose paragraph naming the three mandatory sections
  + audit pointer for canonical template (family-keyed rows,
  multi-identity nesting, AUTHOR_SIGNAL distinction, Tier-2
  revalidationTrigger).
- Reviewer step 2: per-peer "Confirm each peer's APPROVED" replaced
  with family-keyed quorum verification (floor-2 active families +
  non-author APPROVED) + Tier-2 Unresolved-Liveness audit reference.
- Axis 2 paragraph: "100% APPROVED" replaced with "§6.2 quorum
  (canonical: audit pointer above)".
- Reviewer steps 1, 3, 4 preserved (still operational under family-keyed
  rule).
- Empirical-anchor section (lines 209-211) preserved verbatim
  including the historical "before Discussion #11210 reached 100%
  APPROVED" reference — that is a historical event description, not
  a current rule.

Net delta vs origin/dev for pull-request-workflow.md: +112B (under
the 250B oversized-workflow-map cap). Audit file unchanged from
commit a8c083300.

Substrate-quality outcome: §6.1.1 readers now see ONE canonical rule
(audit-delegated, family-keyed) instead of competing inline + audit
shapes.

The Consensus-mandate line (#11217 from #11216) updates from the hardcoded

references remain pointed at the deep substrates (ideation-sandbox-workflow.md

This is the smallest mirror sub of Epic #11796 (single-paragraph edit
landing in AGENTS.md byte budget without restructure). Net file delta:
